edges.description: Local eosinophilic arterial inflammation is followed by intimal hyperplasia and luminal stenosis in affected temporal artery segments.

nodes.description: Affected temporal arteries can show marked intimal hyperplasia, causing luminal stenosis or occlusion. This vascular remodeling explains prominent or nodular temporal arteries and supports surgical excision as both a diagnostic and therapeutic intervention.

evidence.reference_title: Is Kimura's disease associated with juvenile temporal arteritis? A case report and literature review of all juvenile temporal arteritis cases.
evidence.snippet: Histopathology of a biopsy of the left temporal artery revealed inflammatory findings with marked eosinophil infiltration and significant intimal hyperplasia with stenosis of the vascular lumen, indicating JTA.

nodes.description: JTA is a localized eosinophilic arteritis confined to the temporal arteries. Human biopsy series describe inflammatory cells in the arterial wall and perivascular tissues, with eosinophil-rich inflammation rather than the systemic granulomatous large-vessel process typical of older-onset giant cell arteritis.

evidence.reference_title: Vasculitis of the temporal arteries in the young.
evidence.snippet: juvenile temporal arteritis, a localized eosinophilic arteritis confined to the temporal arteries, seems unique to this age group.

evidence.reference_title: Juvenile temporal vasculitis: a rare case in a middle-aged woman.
evidence.snippet: Histologic findings were compatible with JTV (nongranulomatous panarteritis with mononuclear cells and eosinophils).
